zh.lifeSummary: 當Ieuan ap Einion於1334年出生在英國威爾斯卡馬特史爾時，他的父親Maredudd ap Hywel ap Dafydd19歲，母親Mrs. Maredudd ap Hywel ap Dafydd16歲。他與Gwenhwyfer F. Ynyr Fychan在英國威爾斯凱爾納方史爾結婚。他去世於1399年享年65歲。

nameMeanings.name: Einion
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Einion, ap Eynon ‘son of Einion’. See Eynon and compare Bennion . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Eynon
meanings: Welsh: from Enniaun, an old Welsh personal name ultimately derived from Late Latin Annianus, but also associated with Welsh einion ‘anvil’, taken as implying stability and fortitude. See also Beynon and compare Onion .

nameMeanings.name: Ieuan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: When Ieuan ap Einion was born in 1334, in Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Maredudd ap Hywel ap Dafydd, was 19 and his mother, Mrs. Maredudd ap Hywel ap Dafydd, was 16. He married Gwenhwyfer F. Ynyr Fychan in Caernarfonshire, Wales, United Kingdom. He died in 1399, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ieuan ap Einion est né en 1334, à Carmarthenshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Maredudd ap Hywel ap Dafydd, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Maredudd ap Hywel ap Dafydd, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Gwenhwyfer F. Ynyr Fychan à Caernarfonshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé en 1399, à l’âge de 65 ans.

Er hat in Caernarfonshire, Wales, United Kingdom Gwenhwyfer F. Ynyr Fychan geheiratet. Er starb 1399 im Alter von 65 Jahren.

ko.lifeSummary: Ieuan ap Einion(이)가 1334년 영국 웨일스 카마테실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Maredudd ap Hywel ap Dafydd(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Maredudd ap Hywel ap Dafydd(은)는 16세였다. 그는 영국 웨일스 카이른아르폰실르에서 Gwenhwyfer F. Ynyr Fychan(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1399년 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ieuan ap Einion родился в 1334 г. в местности кармартеншир, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Maredudd ap Hywel ap Dafydd было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Maredudd ap Hywel ap Dafydd, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Gwenhwyfer F. Ynyr Fychan в местности каирнарфоншир, Уэльс, Великобритания. Он умер в 1399 г. в возрасте 65 лет.
